summaries.21997: The root cause is that the engine intentionally computes selection rects per glyph/span rather than per line, tracing to an early engine commit; mixed character sets (e.g., the kaomoji string) are broken into alternating spans, making the mismatched heights visible. Engine PR #6335 and #6644 added a new `getBoxesForRange` API with `BoxHeightStyle`/`BoxWidthStyle` parameters enabling line-level rects, but the framework side was never updated to use them, so the bug was incorrectly closed. As of late 2022 the issue is mostly fixed on Android, iOS, and macOS but a slight vertical offset remains on Linux; status is stalled without a framework-side fix.
summaries.181487: Comments confirm the issue on Windows with CJK IME (specifically Chinese input on Windows 10/11 stable 3.35.6). A third-party plugin workaround exists (`force_english_ime: ^0.0.3`), and one user resolved it via direct system API calls. No upstream fix or PR has been proposed; the maintainer asked for a minimal repro of numeric fields triggering IME composition but received no structured response. Status is open with no fix, pending a clearer repro.
summaries.151103: Comments confirm the bug does not reproduce on Android native app with ATOK, indicating it is web-specific. The issue is scoped to Flutter Web with Chrome on Android using the ATOK IME; GBoard does not trigger it. A commenter noted the doubled character also appears in a different font, suggesting it may be reproducible without ATOK by exploiting font fallback. Status is open, assigned to the web team for investigation, no PR.
summaries.90498: Comments clarify the implementation path: the `keyboardKey` trait was originally added for Fuchsia only and is not wired to iOS or Android. On iOS the fix is to set `UIAccessibilityTraitKeyboardKey`; on Android the fix is to call `AccessibilityNodeInfo.setTextEntryKey(true)`. Both are engine-side changes requiring plumbing from the framework. Status is open with no PR, awaiting engine work.
summaries.132577: Comments confirm the existing `ContentInsertionConfiguration` docs already note that `onContentInserted` and `allowedMimeTypes` are Android-only, partially addressing the doc-update request. iOS and web support is acknowledged as desired but unimplemented. No PR or timeline is mentioned. Status is open and stalled with no active work.
summaries.57953: Comments confirm the change is technically straightforward but involves a small breaking change: patterns like `onChanged: condition ? (value) {...} : null` would be affected. A contributor prepared a draft implementation and is awaiting guidance on how to handle the breaking change before proceeding. Status is stalled, pending team decision on the breaking-change approach.
summaries.151876: Comments clarify the request is distinct from issue #29823 and from the existing `TextInputControl` mixin (introduced in PR #76072): the specific pain point is smooth switching between fields that use the native keyboard and fields that use a custom in-app keyboard, including screen resize transitions. The maintainer accepted this scoping and updated the title. Status is open with no PR, kept separate from #29823.
summaries.71083: Comments confirm the bug is distinct from #18761 (which is about read-only Text widgets), and the issue persists through at least Flutter 3.10.6 / Dart 3.0 on macOS. Multiple commenters reproduced it with long URLs in `TextFormField` using `FractionallySizedBox`. No proposed fix or PR exists; status is open and unresolved.
summaries.99848: Comments confirm the issue extends beyond macOS to Flutter Web (reproduced on 3.22.3), where media keys (play/pause) are not detected by `HardwareKeyboard`. No fix, PR, or workaround is mentioned. Status is open and stalled.
summaries.182776: A single substantive comment confirms the black-line rendering artifact and labels it for the framework team to investigate the text selection layout logic. No root cause, PR, or workaround is provided. Status is open, newly triaged.
summaries.56285: Comments add a concrete use case: chip-style multi-select mimicking Gmail's To/CC/BCC field with separator-triggered tokenization. No pub.dev packages of sufficient quality exist. No PR or active implementation work is mentioned. Status is open and stalled.
summaries.84086: Comments link to a related discussion (#80226) proposing a public widget with a `TextSpan` builder and `onChanged` field to decouple the controller from `RenderEditable`. The author clarifies the request is specifically about exposing a public interface/mixin rather than the broader widget proposal. The maintainer acknowledged the direction and noted existing refactoring momentum, referencing #84317 as potentially related. Status is open with no concrete PR.
